edge: Continual Learning models need to be evaluated for trustworthiness and robustness to corruptions.
Existing SOTA CL models are susceptible to various data corruptions during testing.
MetaMix is a framework designed to enhance corruption robustness in Continual Learning through self-adaptive data augmentation.
MetaMix framework optimizes generalization performance against data corruptions during training.
CL corruption datasets are constructed with varying levels of severity to evaluate the corruption robustness of algorithms.
The proposed method demonstrates effectiveness compared to the state-of-the-art baselines in Continual Learning.
Continual learning (CL) aims at imitating human’s ability to learn from non-stationary data distributions without forgetting the previously learned knowledge.
Existing CL systems overlook the robustness against unforeseen data shifts during testing.
For example, the accuracy of DER++ for task-continual learning decreases from 93.9% to 50.5% on split-CIFAR10.
Data corruption can drastically impair the performance of existing image recognition systems.
CL models need to improve their robustness to be trustworthy in real-world scenarios.
Training a CL model to be robust to various corruptions is difficult due to challenges like unseen corruptions and changing data distributions.
Naively augmenting training images with seen corruptions may not help generalize to new ones during testing.
Optimal augmentation strategies are task-dependent and may become less effective on tasks with evolving data distributions.
A memory buffer can store data from previous tasks for augmenting and replaying at later training iterations.
Augmix composes and combines multiple pre-defined augmentation operations with different depths and widths, showing efficacy in improving robustness under traditional supervised classification tasks.
To address unique challenges of corruption-robustness in Continual Learning (CL), the authors propose a temporally self-adaptive Augmix within a meta-learning framework, named MetaMix.
MetaMix adaptively augments the memory buffer data and the currently received new data by learning to mix the augmentation operations tailored to the evolving data distributions.
Memory buffer data is augmented along with the currently received new data in the proposed MetaMix approach.
MetaMix is governed by an LSTM, which inputs context information and outputs the corresponding mixing parameters for the augmentations.
MetaMix ensures the augmentation strategy automatically adapts to non-stationary data distribution.
Extensive experiments on the constructed benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed MetaMix approach compared with several SOTA data-augmentation approaches adapted for CL.
We propose a self-adaptive augmentation method, MetaMix.
To evaluate the corruption robustness of existing and the proposed methods, we propose a new challenging benchmark where various corruptions perturb the testing data of each CL task.

CL settings can be categorized into three scenarios based on whether task identities of the testing data are available to the CL model during test time
task-continual learning studies the scenario that the task identities are available during testing and is the easiest learning setting
domain incremental learning focuses on the case that all the tasks during CL solve the same classification task with different data distribution and the task identities are unknown during testing
class-continual learning focuses on the scenario that task identities are unavailable and is the most difficult learning setting

AugMix maintains augmented data diversity by mixing several augmentation chains to enhance model performance.
AugMix uses an augmentation operation set O during training to create diverse augmentation chains for improved model performance.
The augmented image is a convex mix of A chains, denoted by the mixing weights w, where the mixing weights w is sampled from a distribution.
AugMix combines the mixed image with the original image through another random convex combination to obtain the final image.
With the mixed image xaug, AugMix combines it with the original image through another random convex combination to obtain the final image xaugmix.
AugMix adopts a fixed random augmentation policy using Dirichlet and Beta distributions during the entire training process.
AugMix uses Jensen Shannon divergence for regularizing the output consistency between the original and augmented data.

The CL Corruption Benchmark introduces new datasets named Split-CIFAR10-C, Split-CIFAR100-C, and Split-miniImageNet-C to evaluate the generalization of CL models to unseen corruptions during testing.
Split-CIFAR-10-C splits the CIFAR-10 dataset into 5 disjoint tasks, each task containing 2 classes.
Split-CIFAR-100-C splits the CIFAR-100 dataset into 10 disjoint tasks, each task containing 10 classes.
Split-miniImageNet-C splits the miniImageNet dataset into 10 disjoint tasks, each task containing 10 classes.
